content.rendered:
Here is an entry from my Art book upcoming…soon to be available I’m planning, online. The plan is to release the first two volumes first. I’m typing them up presently. Here is the excerpt.

3/15/25

Back to sticking to a method. Its then important to stay healthy stay positive and not get overwrought if it doesn’t work right off the bat. Its like a pair of shoes I tried on recently. I thought ‘too tight’ then the revelation ‘just right actually’, but then the third day, or whatever day it was, i realised they weren’t too short but too snug, as in my toes were touching the tops of the shoes not the end of the shoes – but day two felt so different from day one and day three from day two. The point is just because it doesn’t work one day keep at it. You have to bear these things out. If someone had given me all these tips that I’ve enumerated I dont know what i’d do but i would most likely give up  on a few if they didn’t immediately work . There’s really too much to (art) in my opinion to (learn by) going to some art school or even trying to apply everything or thereabouts or even half the stuff in here; you’ll be overwhelmed . I still am…

title.rendered: Things an artist should have part two

content.rendered:
This is sort of a boring topic, I’m starting to think but I’ll go on anyway.
It took me several years of getting paint on my hands among other places to realize I really should be wearing gloves, so that is what I did. I use the kind as you can see in my videos, you’d get at hardware stores for around $4.85 or maybe more. I don’t know what they’re called but they are most likely called workman’s gloves. I put on one at a time, as in I only put on one, for the time I’m painting. That is, on my hand that holds the paint brush and holds the paint and tries to unscrew the bottle. These bottles by the way, Harding if you are seeing this are hard to unscrew/unlodge/ take off the top especially while in the middle of painting when you impatiently try to. But yes the gloves have saved my hands over the years. Gloves on both hands is ideal actually but wearing one on the painting hand usually suffices.

excerpt.rendered: This is sort of a boring topic, I’m starting to think but I’ll go on anyway. It took me several years of getting paint on my hands among other places to realize I really should be wearing gloves, so that is what I did. I use the kind as you can see in my videos, […]

title.rendered: Things you should have as an artist Part 1

content.rendered:
One thing you should have as an artist..a change of pants. you never know when youre going or where youre going to get paint. It ends up in the darndest places..I don’t know how but I get it on the seat of my pants! So when I leave the painting area I change my pants, or I try to, or at least I just did that now.
Better yet, its better to have a smock, ideally one that covers your pants if possible.. So really you should have a smock among other things.

excerpt.rendered: One thing you should have as an artist..a change of pants. you never know when youre going or where youre going to get paint. It ends up in the darndest places..I don’t know how but I get it on the seat of my pants! So when I leave the painting area I change my pants, […]

title.rendered: Deepseek suggests I post three times a week

content.rendered:
Among the things discussed with deepseek, whose guidance I sought regarding tik tok was how often one should post there in order to generate a larger audience. To this deepseek advised at least three times a week and gave specific times of day when one should in order for ones videos to be seen by the most people. I actually forget what times they suggested, something along the lines of Monday morning was one; either before work or during. But for me I was already too lost in the thought of how would I begin to be able to do three videos in a week and isn’t that an insane level of commitment, to pay any further attention to what he was saying. But I should mention that he/she/whatever it is advised to film several scenes all at once, which I think is a good idea, so that one would more efficiently prosecute such an undertaking, which again I think is good. But good or not, is it even in that way still doable? As in, yes sure I could for a few weeks keep it up but then what about for months and months on end? And would I want to do it in the first place?
This is what I’m thinking over at present. I do think however that one video per week doesn’t seem like enough even to me. Perhaps with my new setup and the three or more at a time idea, it won’t be such a chore!

excerpt.rendered: Among the things discussed with deepseek, whose guidance I sought regarding tik tok was how often one should post there in order to generate a larger audience. To this deepseek advised at least three times a week and gave specific times of day when one should in order for ones videos to be seen by […]

title.rendered: New style videos coming soon on Tok

content.rendered:
I’m, thanks to deepseek to whom I’m deeply in debt or somewhat in debt, now in the process of having come up with a new scheme for filming my instructional videos which go now on Tok but may in the future go to other places. That setup is consistent of a Sony camcorder and tripod..among other things. Those other things being an easel and a computer for editing purposes. On the computer I’m now switching to davinci resolve, although I hadn’t used the computer prior at all for tik tok but I had done for other video projects. Now you might ask ‘ what other places??’ as in ‘where might I find them??’ these video, if ‘..I don’t go there?’ Well I’m thinking of YouTube, or some other venue such as this site here. I don’t really know yet. I like YouTube, due to the ease of uploading a file and then embedding it, the fast playback rate but i don’t like other things about it. For example…
Anyway the point is that I should rather soon have some new videos in a new style up very soon!

That setup is consistent of a Sony camcorder and tripod..among […]

content.rendered:
I have a good one from the book I’m working on which I hope to also be working on typing this July. Its a book on my artistic journey to some extent but also has included or actually almost entirely is artistic technique I’ve developed over the years. I was thinking about serializing it here in a special blog-like section.

I’m not sure how I’ll end up having it yet but like I say I hope to finish it and type of my sloppy hand written writings this summer. I will then locate the artist statement therein and put it here as my artist statement.

Artist statements never really made sense to me as far as I go; I don’t have a whole lot to state essentially. I just let it happen more or less and I think that this entry in my book (which is composed of entries almost entirely except for the intro) somewhat explains that in a more verbose sort of way if I remember correctly. I do remember thinking it was pretty good at the time and that it might be good as an artists’ statement.

content.rendered:
Lessons

Im offering painting lessons to up to three people at a time. It will be on Zoom or live in person depending on where you are. Im in Arlington VA. The first/next session will be on 5/1 and 5/2 noon EDT for a half hour. This is more for beginners where I can offer pointers and show some of my techniques. Click here to register! Once you do I will contact you with more info. If you have any questions email me at steve.breighner@gmail.com

content.rendered:
Paints

I use a lot of different brands and colors..Ill add some more photos later but the main ones used would be these below.
Here are the main colors I typically use and the brands Im currently using:

  1. Ivory Black (Utrecht)
  2. Cadmium Red (Utrecht)
  3. Cadmium Yellow (Utrecht)
  4. Burnt Umber (Michael Harding)
  5. Phthalocyanine Blue (Michael Harding)
  6. Greeen (Utrecht)
  7. Titianium WHite (Utrecht)
  8. Creminitz White (Michael Harding)

content.rendered:
March 20 2023

Welcome to my artist’s blog! This is where I’ll be sharing my thoughts, ideas, and inspiration with you. As an artist, I’m always exploring new techniques, experimenting with different mediums, and pushing myself to create something new and exciting. On this blog, I’ll be sharing my latest works, discussing my artistic process, and giving you a behind-the-scenes look at my creative life. Whether you’re an artist yourself or just love art, I hope you’ll find something here to inspire you. So grab a cup of coffee and let’s dive into the world of art together!

also Im still working on the illustrations page..that will be more normal style artwork
